droppedBalls.name: Gallatin
droppedBalls.note: Dropped from prep doc. Last status: Leaving here to talk about if we have time. I think Carson said he sent you the notes of our conversation - just wanted to talk through everything. The brokers have followed up with him, so just wanted to keep it on all of our radar. It seems far-fetched/unlikely in my opinion. MacBid I had an update call with one of the brokers to check in. Sounds like business is going well, and they are primarily looking for the more industrial-style locations for their expansion. I am checking back in with them this week because all of the major contracts were supposed to be awarded last week, and that will dictate what markets they are looking at for expansion. Just wanted to close the loop from previous convos. Fostr Yardi is tied in. Roll out to the team coming soon. I'll show you some examples in our meeting.

yourPlate.text: Buena Vista / Moda Vista group call — Micah Slack-DMed a Nashville Business Journal-style note that Moda Homes closed a $49.43M construction loan from Forman Capital for Moda Vista, a 102-unit build-to-rent community at 3320 Curtis St in Bordeaux/NW Nashville, and said Jacob needs to call this group about Buena Vista. Command Center updated

Village at Hardin Valley / Tanner Sanford off-market opportunity — Tanner Sanford surfaced an off-market Village at Hardin Valley opportunity; Command Center was updated. Jacob needs to review whether this is worth pursuing or forwarding internally. No Gmail draft was created

PCON / Michael donation thank-you and grant designation check — Sam High forwarded a new HubSpot donation that came in almost immediately after the co-investment email. Michael chose the general donation option; Sam suggested Jacob/Chris thank him and ask whether he wanted the dollars directed to a specific grant
PCON / Sam High retreat-dinner availability poll — Sam High sent a PCON retreat/dinner availability poll; Jacob needs to respond with availability or preference. No Gmail draft was created

PCON / Carson West outreach suggested reply — Communication Triage delivered suggested reply text for Carson West / PCON outreach; Jacob needs to review and handle manually if he wants to respond. No Gmail draft was created

Hannah Hart / Relay error follow-up — Communication Triage flagged a Hannah Hart Relay error alert in the email channel; Jacob needs to review the alert and decide whether any system/vendor follow-up is needed

Chea J. / Dixie Village Crexi floorplan request — Chea J. requested the floorplan for Suite 2591 at 2549 W Franklin Blvd through Crexi. Suggested reply posted in Discord asking for best phone/email and offering to send the floorplan. No Gmail draft was created and no external reply was sent

Emerson / Gallatin Fat Bottom space repairs and walkthrough follow-up — Trace Morris replied on Slack after Jacob asked about the new tenant walkthrough/fire-marshal context. Trace said broken exit signs and other items have been fixed; the roof hole had been raised to Josh/Micah, Josh did not want Anchor to pay and assumed Fat Bottom would, but Fat Bottom did not; Trace will inspect Monday and may be able to fix it himself. Door handles can be bought/replaced because Fat Bottom removed them when closing the downstairs bar. 6/22 update: Trace said the downstairs event-space leak is fixed; plumber will return tomorrow to turn the water back on and double-check it. Trace asked Jacob to send any list of walkthrough items the tenant provides

Carole Ward / Nashville IT issue reminder — Carole first Slack-DMed asking, “Were you able to get the Concur invoices resolved?” then clarified at 6 PM that she meant Nashville IT, not Concur. Jacob needs to respond/update her on what Nashville IT item needs resolution and status

Montgomery Martial Arts / Virginia College Plaza inquiry — Watkins Johnston IV emailed that he is working with Montgomery Martial Arts on a relocation needing roughly 3,000 SF open layout at 6132 Atlanta Hwy / Virginia College SC and asked whether Anchor has anything that works plus pricing. Suggested reply posted in Discord; no Gmail draft was created. Command Center added the lead as Montgomery Martial Arts

Natalie T. / Dixie Village Crexi lead — Natalie requested the Dixie Village property and more info through Crexi; Communication Triage posted suggested reply text asking for best phone/email, business type, and square footage, and pushed the deal update to Command Center. Jacob needs to handle/send manually if he wants to pursue; no Gmail draft was created

Zee Nails / Pea Ridge buyer creditworthiness review — Mathison emailed that a buyer wants to purchase Zee Nails for $25K, previously owned three Illinois nail salons, has recent tax returns showing only about $9K-$12K income after expenses, has cash from selling his house, wants to invest $60K-$70K in the space, and wants to extend the current lease from 2029 to 2034. Jacob needs to help Mathison think through buyer creditworthiness and lease/assignment posture. 6/23 5 PM update: Mathison Slack-DMed asking whether Jacob has looked at the Zee Nails sale info because he needs to get back to the buyer

Flavors of India / Maple Row HVAC responsibility — Melissa forwarded Girish's HVAC/AC follow-up and asked Jacob for thoughts; tenant is asking how much the building owner will contribute, while Melissa says prior emails indicate the tenant is responsible for HVAC. Jacob needs to advise Melissa on responsibility/contribution posture before she responds

Little Caesars / Hendersonville Maple Row inquiry — Tiffanie Fitzpatrick emailed that her client Little Caesars is looking for space in Hendersonville and asked whether the Fox's Pizza space is available, plus any other options. Jacob replied with Maple Row flyer/photos, noted the Fox's Pizza space has a lease pending, offered the adjacent 2,244 SF suite, and quoted mid-to-upper $20s as-is with $4.41 NNN. 6/22 update: Tiffanie asked due-diligence questions for a global pizza chain: grease trap access, whether electrical is at least 400 amps, gas/gas-line size, water-line size, and why Fox's Pizza is moving out. Suggested reply posted in Discord; no Gmail draft was created. Command Center updated

Gilded Spine / Maple Row renewed interest — Hillary Holmes re-engaged after prior tour/TI concerns and asked whether the Maple Row space is already leased; she is open to chatting again. 6/23 update: Hillary followed up after texting Jacob and asked to confirm Hendersonville Planning/zoning and Certificate of Occupancy feasibility for retail + limited-service restaurant use, plus rewalk the space with her facilities manager to review utilities/plumbing/flooring. Suggested reply posted in Discord; no Gmail draft was created

Teriyaki Madness / Pell City inquiry — Robert Cook replied that Teriyaki Madness may work with a hybrid landlord work-letter/TI structure, typically vanilla shell/electrical plus TI, after Jacob sent Pell City availability. Jacob needs to decide whether/how to keep pursuing for the demised gym space

312 Wilson Pike / new broker inquiries — 2026-06-15 Communication Triage flagged fresh broker interest from Ashley Cusker for a dance studio; Ross Hopkins for an athletic-training business needing 5,000-10,000 SF in Brentwood/Franklin; Mission Pet Health / Your Pet Dentist via Neal NeSmith and Duane Roggendorff, seeking roughly 4,500-5,500 SF / ~5,000 SF for a national-credit veterinary dental specialty relocation by March 2027 and asking whether the building can be subdivided; and Brandon Hoop reporting Devin/Redline has interest after touring and asking whether to send an LOI. Jacob handled Ross on 6/15: sent flyer/info, coordinated with Adam, and scheduled the tour for Wednesday 6/17 at 12:00 PM with Adam meeting them onsite; Ross acknowledged. 6/15 leasing meeting added strong Wilson relisting traction: Brandon/personal-training client may want front half, Ross Brown daycare client is looking for 10k-12k SF but likely parking/outdoor-space challenged, and competing interest needs to be handled against Ryder/Borders LOI/walkthrough path. 6/17 update: Ross Hopkins / Southeast Venture submitted a signed Futbol Factory LOI for the front portion of the building after touring; Jon Speers / Equitable separately asked whether the listing is available, lease rate, interior photos, clear heights, and other details. 6/19 update: Ryder Ballard followed up on the Borders/Ryder LOI asking whether Jacob/Adam have questions. 6/22 update: Brandon Hoop sent signed and Word LOI versions for the front half of 312 Wilson Pike from Devin/Redline. 6/23 2 PM update: Noble Judy / CBRE asked whether Anchor would sell Wilson Pike and requested pricing guidance; Adam separately relayed by Slack that Noble asked for the ALTA/title material and mentioned possible purchase/offers. 6/23 5 PM update: Ross Hopkins / Southeast Venture followed up with Futbol Factory profit and loss information and a backup LOI for the rear portion of 312 Wilson Pike, saying the tenant would take either the front or rear space depending on landlord preference. Jacob still needs to decide response/subdivision/LOI/sale posture across the Wilson interests. No Gmail drafts were created

Shelbyville former Big Lots redevelopment/outparcel track — 6/15 leasing meeting advanced a separate redevelopment path beyond the Ryan Jordan info request: call Indiana engineer/contact or Nashville referral to confirm local Shelbyville/city experience; ask for local engineering recommendations; call city/economic development to learn which fast-food/c-store users have been looking; sketch/test an outparcel concept with c-store, restaurant, Dutch Bros/Seven Brew-type small user, curb cuts, subdivision constraints, and whether teardown/ground leases outperform leasing the 40k SF box. 6/17 Dutch Bros/Ethan Flynn showed interest: needs about 0.4 acre, prefers BTS/someone fee-develops, with GL plus high TIA as alternative/reverse BTS, and indicated rent tolerance around $150K. 6/22 meeting: start C-store/QSR outreach now rather than waiting for perfect engineering; send survey/plot info to prospects; research regional Indy/Shelbyville fuel users; ask Carson for his Montgomery C-store/outparcel outreach list; set up Dutch Bros call and clarify TI/BTS/reverse-BTS economics

Bealls / Shelbyville former Big Lots updated term sheet — Paul Gold sent an updated term sheet for a portion of the former Big Lots in Shelbyville, IN after Vegas/subsequent conversations; revised to NNN rent rather than last year's gross deal and increased TI from $350K to $400K over VBox delivery. Jacob needs to review the attached DOCX term sheet and call Paul if he wants to discuss. Do not process the DOCX in the main session

Leasing phone tree / missed lead callback fix — update main office voicemail/phone tree so leasing callers are told to hang up and call the direct leasing number; routed calls without callback numbers show only the office number and can lose leads. Adam’s phone is now connected to the leasing line

312 Wilson Pike / KWC-Skiptown dog boarding inquiry — KWC/Jerry/Cynthia asked about dog boarding/daycare/grooming use for 312 Wilson Pike; Communication Triage posted suggested reply text in Discord asking whether this is for Skiptown directly and target opening timeline. No Gmail draft was created; Jacob needs to approve/manual-send if he wants to pursue

312 Wilson Pike / Ryder Ballard LOI — Ryder/Borders Family Office LOI is in hand for the whole building at $16/SF, $10k TI, 5-year term plus two 1-year options; use sounds like shared-services office/warehouse for portfolio service businesses rather than a true family office. 6/22 meeting: keep momentum but tighten LOI/lease language hard before commitment: require financials, define exact LL work, avoid vague “reasonable condition” / floor-leveling obligations, confirm greenhouse/removal scope, check Brentwood permit/sprinkler triggers, ask whether tenant/contractor intends to pull permits, and cap Anchor’s total TI/capital exposure around $300K rather than open-ended add-ons. Jacob needs to review/respond and coordinate counter posture while weighing competing Redline/Futbol Factory/front-half paths

Wingstop / McMinnville lease draft review — Kevin Trujillo / Sarah Langan sent lease draft attachment `Lease-Wingstop McMinnville Plaza.docx`; Jacob needs to review/route for legal review. Command Center moved Wingstop to Lease Draft & Review

Put HSBC on the market — Jacob asked in #tasks to put HSBC on the market. Interpreted as Huntington School of Beauty Culture / HSBC at Pea Ridge based on Fostr tenant record: Pea Ridge, suite PEARID-O, 7,104.17 SF, current rent $2,154.17/mo. Prepare/update listing materials and market the space

Put Dance Hendersonville on the market — Jacob asked in #tasks to put Dance Hendersonville on the market. Fostr tenant record: Maple Row suite MAPLER-350, 3,481 SF, current rent $6,085.95/mo. Prepare/update listing materials and market the space

Girish Kumar / Nothing But Noodles Maple Row Art Studio inquiry — Girish followed up on Hendersonville Nutrition/Maple Row and said he has a Nothing But Noodles franchise concept for the Art Studio suite; he also asked what business is going into Fox's Pizza. Gmail draft reply loaded for Jacob approval saying Nothing But Noodles could be interesting for the Art Studio suite, Fox's is not fully wrapped up enough to share details, and asking timing for Nothing But Noodles. Marked unread/actioned for visibility. Do not send unless Jacob approves

Marlon Rodriguez / Dixie Village LoopNet lead — prospect asked about Dixie Village and indicated 0-4,000 SF range; Gmail draft reply loaded for Jacob approval asking for business type. Do not send unless Jacob approves

Amin Lakhani / Smart LED TN LLC Highland lease application — lease application received for Highland Ave Jackson TN; proposed use is business expansion, requested 5-year term, and supporting financial PDFs were listed in the Jotform submission. Carson shared a Google Doc on Slack with changes in yellow and deletions in red and offered to review today or tomorrow. 6/8 meeting: Micah/Jacob viewed the tenant positively, want the deal done, and prefer monthly/net framing over confusing PSF language. 6/15 meeting: tenant appears agreed after revised economics; Jacob needs to update lease and text DocuSign, while refusing tenant-requested ROFR language. 6/17 update: Amin replied to Jacob's revised lease draft with proposed changes on expanded permitted use, broad subletting without prior consent, one-year guaranty burnoff, no ongoing financial reporting, flexible signage updates, and 50/50 HVAC/plumbing/electrical cost sharing. 6/22 meeting: do not accept corporate-only / one-year guaranty burnoff without corporate financials; likely require personal guaranty through initial 5-year term, possibly with later burn-down, and ask Ashby to review Anchor’s guaranty form for collectability/credit-report leverage. Text/send online document link because Amin responds better by text

Carson Knight / Dashlane account recovery — Carson has a pending Dashlane Account Recovery request and cannot log in until an admin approves it. Needs Jacob/admin approval; do not approve or change account access without explicit permission

Katelyn Thompson / New Memphis Suite 106 lease application — Katelyn completed the Anchor lease application after Jacob sent the virtual tour and form link; Gmail draft reply loaded for Jacob approval saying Jacob will review everything and circle back with next steps. Marked unread/actioned for visibility. Do not send unless Jacob approves

Jennifer Stewart / Maple Row leasing inquiry — Jennifer asked about available suites, rent, opex, co-tenancy, site plans, and leasing materials for Sparkle Dog Grooming and Pigtails & Crewcuts. Gmail draft reply loaded for Jacob approval saying Jacob will check current availability, rent, and opex and circle back. Marked unread/actioned for visibility. Do not send unless Jacob approves

Swig / Sevierville exclusive review — run the Sevierville lease/exclusive language through Claude/Jarvis before outreach; Micah/Jacob think Swig is interesting but likely blocked by coffee/drink exclusive language

Richard Burt broker lunch / email-list opportunity — get on Richard Burt's broker lunch/email list and attend the next lunch if workable; Micah wants Jacob/Adam using it for broker reps and Madison context
Gallatin / Trader Joe's parking message — tell the brokers Anchor is pulling for them, but the concept does not work if it requires giving up the front/key hotel parking or frontage area

Juany Almanza / Dixie Village Mexican bakery LoopNet follow-up — after Jacob asked whether the $140,000 was on hand and what size suite she needed, prospect replied that she has the money and wants 1,300-1,600 SF. Gmail draft reply loaded for Jacob approval saying he will review with ownership and circle back. Review concept/business plan and decide whether to discuss with ownership given existing Mexican bakery at Dixie Village. Do not send unless Jacob approves

Omar Hidais / Pea Ridge LoopNet follow-up — after Jacob asked whether this would be Omar's first location, Omar replied that he operates several Tobacco/Vape shops across multiple states and is also a landlord; on 2026-06-05 he asked what spaces are available and what the asking rent is. Gmail draft reply loaded saying Jacob will check current availability/asking rent at Pea Ridge and get back to him. Do not send unless Jacob approves

Factory Connection / Russellville availability check — Justin Rains said Russellville remains on Factory Connection's target list for October openings and asked whether Anchor has any new openings there; Gmail draft reply loaded saying Jacob will check available space and get back to him. Do not send unless Jacob approves

Brandon Hoop / 144 Wilson Dr. Kemp zoning follow-up — Brandon said C4 zoning initially looks workable for Dr. Kemp's use but recommended confirming with Brentwood planning before finalizing; Gmail draft reply loaded saying Jacob will work on city confirmation and circle back. Do not send unless Jacob approves

Robert Henson / Big Lots Evansville concept follow-up — Robert sent details on Evansville Rehab's planned $25M facility, capital campaign, and possible use of the Big Lots property for Adult Day Services / supportive programming; Gmail draft reply loaded saying Jacob will review internally and circle back. 6/8 meeting: build as-is / landlord-funded TI scenarios, push toward a 10-year term, show the tenant how TI amortization works, and use a ROFR instead of a fixed purchase option. Where Ya Bin is paused for at least the next few months. 6/18 update: Robert said Evansville Rehab was touring at 3 PM with a donor who is also a developer, still had alarm codes if needed, and really likes Anchor's proposal while testing what they can make work. 6/19 update: after the tour, Robert asked what it would cost to purchase the building outright. Jacob needs to decide sale-price posture versus lease/TI structure and respond. Do not send unless Jacob approves

Jesse Warhurst / Russellville building 16 follow-up — Jesse sent Shop Jesse K website info, then completed the lease application for Russellville on 2026-06-08. Proposed use is Boutique Storefront, entity is a sole proprietorship, requested lease term is 1-2 years, projected opening is July 1, and phone is 256-436-5984. 6/17 update: Jacob told Jesse a new key was being placed in the lockbox; Mathison then confirmed the Suite 16 key with green tag is in the box beside the Direct Auto Insurance / old Republican HQ location, code 4046. 6/18 update: Jesse toured and said the space is perfect for their use but needs flooring, baseboards, paint, lighting, ceiling tile, hole repair, and toilet work; he asked for one or two months free so they can fix these items. 6/22 meeting: Micah is open to giving Jesse a shot if Anchor spends little/no money; small rent is better than vacancy, but keep landlord spend very tight. Jacob needs to respond on free-rent/repair posture. No new Gmail draft was created
Send Malone flyer to small shop tenants — carried forward in latest reaction card; still depends on finished flyer
Finish Malone flyer — carried forward in latest reaction card
Blast out Buena Vista listings — Jacob re-upped in 5/8 brain dump: send Buena Vista out to all those people, including Derek Morgan from Micah email
Buena Vista sign — pick a handyman to go do/install the Buena Vista sign; previously framed as working with Parker on the sign
Ask Buena Vista land contacts for prior bid materials and move the sod / site presentation work forward — carried forward per reaction card item 6

Call Diego re Buena Vista purchase inquiry - texted from Approver after seeing Craigslist Nashville listing, wants to discuss purchase price and site conditions. Call back: 561-517-6728. Carried forward per reaction card item 7
Send Buena Vista to Millrose and Lennar — carried forward per reaction card item 8; fold into broader Buena Vista send-out list including Derek Morgan from Micah email
Pell City — pressure wash or repaint facade; recent photos made the frontage look rough. Carried forward per reaction card item 9

Respond to Pell City Outparcel LOI — Ellianos Coffee LOI received from Wallace Schmuck (ws@shanwalt.com, 210-464-7707) / Shannon Waltchack. Latest reaction fallback says carry forward rather than Monday. Call Wallace or Keith with feedback when Jacob re-prioritizes
Camile C. (Crexi) — Buena Vista Pike JV/participation inquiry deferred per closeout; do not chase now. Reopen only if Jacob decides to pursue JV/participation conversation
Jason Jones — Malone Card Show inquiry, short-term 8,000 SF event rental at Malone. Carried forward per reaction card item 14
Update New Memphis lease late fee language to $100 or 5%, whichever is greater — carried forward per reaction card item 15

Shoe Dept (Malone) - lock construction timeline and lease-obligation map with Clay to avoid opening-year slippage. 6/19 update: Jennifer Arnold / Design Team sent the Shoe Dept Encore Malone brand book PDF for approval; Jacob needs to review/approve or comment. Command Center updated

Pell City Physical Therapy / Home Health RFP — Challenge/value-engineer RFP pricing; 5/11 meeting added that the original home-health company is back and may slot into the middle space, so keep competitive pressure across the Pell City prospects. Also tell Easterseals/Laurie another interested party is active and ask for CEO feedback within 10-14 days. 6/18 update: Tom Fidler/MacKenzie forwarded a DOCX commercial lease for Pell City Partners / Upstream Growth (Drayer PT / Upstream Rehabilitation); Jacob needs to review/route lease comments and not process the DOCX in the main session

Emerson / Lately Projects — get lease signed this week; Jacob said Anchor has legal standing on expiration timing and should proceed, with construction storefront height details to be worked out after signing

Hamilton vacancy / laundromat — Jacob re-upped in 5/8 brain dump: do the laundromat to-do from the leasing meeting by contacting prior laundromat contact and floating whether $200K-$300K TI would move the needle, with better rent required. 6/8 meeting: Hamilton needs action beyond Spirit; hustle 10K-15K SF users, and be careful underwriting box splits because rear/dead space may have little or no rent value

Adam onboarding / leasing cadence — Jacob re-upped in 5/8 brain dump: work on Adam's onboarding plan with early Slack/email access, leasing channel history, podcasts/lease self-study, early real outreach, weekly Jacob/Adam one-on-one, and continued Jacob/Micah active-deal meeting. 5/11 meeting: Adam likely full-time Nashville next week / June 1 formal start still likely; build in onboarding time. 5/27 Adam materials review completed: 56 Leasing Weekly Activity docs reviewed, with 16 safe, 36 needing redaction, and 4 do-not-share-raw; next step is cleaned Adam-safe copies/summaries if Jacob asks. 6/8 meeting: set a couple recurring check-ins with Adam, but require him to bring researched recommendations/options rather than raw questions; revenue opportunities can interrupt by Slack/call, otherwise batch for Monday. 6/15 meeting: Adam’s phone is connected to the leasing line; plan is for him to take all leasing calls this week and study Jacob's property/vacancy screen recordings, including nuance on functionally available spaces

MacBid / Evansville-Hamilton expansion — Jacob pinged CBRE/MacBid for ICSC; Carmine replied 5/12 PM that Collin is no longer on the MAC account, James Stocker is now the main TM lead, and they will not be at ICSC this year. 6/8 meeting: broker feedback suggests growth skews toward cheaper warehouse/pickup formats, though recent contracts appear generally on plan; watch renewal risk and start listing/replacement planning about 18 months before expirations if warehouse strategy threatens retail box renewals

Five Below / Malone kickoff follow-up — Desiree Warren sent EC markups 5/18 asking for facade renderings/adjacent-tenant context, roof/drainage/water-damage feedback, rear CMU sealing confirmation, sprinkler/meter-room access + leaseline adjustment approval, and confirmation whether LL will require sprinklers; Dawn Baker is replacing Brian as Five Below construction PM. Review/respond with Clay

Five Below / Malone landlord drawings — decide with Clay/ownership whether to start landlord plan set before lease execution; scope includes facade, demising, loading, and RTU openings. 6/17 update: Clay asked whether to commission facade/elevation architectural drawings now or wait until Five Below's plans arrive and StoreCrafters can bid for hard numbers; Clay recommends waiting because the architect proposal is not a small amount to risk before lease feasibility is confirmed. 6/18 update: Desiree Warren followed up again asking for storefront/facade design guidance and any information on what Anchor's team is thinking, so the facade/CD decision is now pressing

Five Below / Malone lease exhibits — Five Below legal returned clean and redline lease versions on 2026-06-18 at 4:50 PM; Ashby sent handwritten comments on 2026-06-23 at 7:48 PM with checkmarks/OKs, a few pushback items, and several `JD` items for Jacob/Micah to confirm. Jacob needs to review work letter, final sign exhibit, no-build-area exhibit, Ashby comments, and route legal/business responses

UPS Store / McMinnville — LOI is signed/returned and UPS now has full site approval; Jon Petty asked whether Anchor can get a lease draft soon. Move toward lease draft/REC path. 5/11 meeting clarified UPS and Wingstop are not requiring facade work, just hoping for it, so do not promise upgrades until pricing/value-engineering is known

James Rainer / McMinnville Plaza behavioral-health RFP — James followed up on the national outpatient substance-abuse treatment client/RFP for Suites 105/106 after providing use details for landlord review. Jacob needs to present the behavioral-health use/RFP to ownership or reply with status/questions
McMinnville landscaping — active landscaper/weeds follow-up per Monday sync; no longer passive watch
Crunch Fitness / McMinnville Suite 107 — follow up/send floor plan reply; moved from stale WATCHING to active DO per Monday sync
Cleveland Furniture Bank / 6282 Pearl Road — Gmail draft reply loaded for Jacob approval; draft says Jacob will get back up to speed and call Tom. Do not send unless Jacob approves

dropped.name: Gallatin
dropped.lastStatus: Leaving here to talk about if we have time. I think Carson said he sent you the notes of our conversation - just wanted to talk through everything. The brokers have followed up with him, so just wanted to keep it on all of our radar. It seems far-fetched/unlikely in my opinion. MacBid I had an update call with one of the brokers to check in. Sounds like business is going well, and they are primarily looking for the more industrial-style locations for their expansion. I am checking back in with them this week because all of the major contracts were supposed to be awarded last week, and that will dictate what markets they are looking at for expansion. Just wanted to close the loop from previous convos. Fostr Yardi is tied in. Roll out to the team coming soon. I'll show you some examples in our meeting.

progressed.to: Wingstop sent us a lease We sent the UPS store a lease. We have the site visit with Aldi this week. I will prep some packets that have information on the space. Also, I just realized that Royal Furniture doesn't open until 10:00 AM so I'll reach out to them and see if we can get in earlier. If not, I will coordinate with Sheldon so we tell them to meet at 10 instead of 9. Also, I think Melissa D is going to tag along with us since she's never seen the property.

progressed.name: Smart LED
progressed.from: We sent a lease out for the Highland Vacancy but all still subject to review/change. It is an LED lighting store. They have a store in Memphis, and this would be their second location. (https://slsmem.com/, https://share.google/8PEr9DXG3wPMIsY7Q)
progressed.to: In typical fashion, once getting the tenant lease, they have been negotiating more on the rent. I was texting back and forth with him this weekend. As of last night, we had agreed on terms, and I just need to edit the lease and send it back to them. The short version is we changed some of the rent bumps for the initial term but got 2% annual increases for any of the options.

updated.name: Evansville Big Lots
updated.from: (Jarvis summary) Robert Henson from Evansville Rehab says they are building a new $25M therapy facility and are looking at the Big Lots box for Adult Day Services, potentially on a 3-5 year lease with an option to buy. Their contractor's rough estimate was $6M for a full renovation or about half that for half the building, and Robert asked for lease-rate scenarios with them funding the work versus us providing some renovation support.
updated.to: I sent them the options for a 5/10-year option at both $1M and $2M in TI. I'll keep you posted.
